q: Three less than four times a number is 25. What is the number?
explain: Translate to algebra: 4x − 3 = 25. Add 3 to both sides: 4x = 28. Divide by 4: x = 7.

q: A car travels 180 miles in 3 hours at a constant speed. What is its speed in miles per hour?
explain: Using d = rt, speed r = d/t = 180/3 = 60 mph.

q: The sum of three consecutive integers is 72. What is the largest of the three integers?
explain: Let the integers be x, x+1, x+2. Then 3x + 3 = 72, so 3x = 69 and x = 23. The integers are 23, 24, 25; the largest is 25.

q: Maria is 3 times as old as her son. The sum of their ages is 48. How old is Maria?
explain: Let the son's age be s. Then 3s + s = 48, so 4s = 48 and s = 12. Maria is 3(12) = 36.

q: Machine X can complete a job alone in 6 hours, and Machine Y can complete the same job alone in 3 hours. Working together at their constant rates, how many hours will they take to complete the job?
explain: Combined rate = 1/6 + 1/3 = 1/6 + 2/6 = 3/6 = 1/2 job per hour. Time = 1 / (1/2) = 2 hours.

q: A chemist has 8 liters of a 25% acid solution. How many liters of a 50% acid solution must be added to produce a mixture that is 40% acid?
explain: Let x be the liters of 50% solution added. Acid balance: 0.25(8) + 0.50x = 0.40(8 + x). So 2 + 0.5x = 3.2 + 0.4x, giving 0.1x = 1.2 and x = 12 liters.

q: A driver covers 120 miles at 40 miles per hour, then returns over the same route at 60 miles per hour. What is the average speed, in miles per hour, for the entire round trip?
explain: Average speed = total distance / total time. Total distance = 240 miles. Time = 120/40 + 120/60 = 3 + 2 = 5 hours. Average speed = 240/5 = 48 mph. (Note it is not the simple mean of 40 and 60.)

q: The sum of four consecutive even integers is 100. What is the greatest of these integers?
explain: Let the integers be x, x+2, x+4, x+6. Then 4x + 12 = 100, so 4x = 88 and x = 22. The integers are 22, 24, 26, 28; the greatest is 28.

info: Train A travels 360 miles in 5 hours at a constant speed. Train B travels 280 miles in 4 hours at a constant speed.
qa: The constant speed of Train A
qb: The constant speed of Train B
explain: Train A speed = 360/5 = 72 mph. Train B speed = 280/4 = 70 mph. Since 72 > 70, Quantity A is greater.

q: Three pipes fill a tank at constant rates: pipe A alone in 4 hours, pipe B alone in 6 hours, and pipe C alone in 12 hours. If all three pipes are opened together, how many hours will it take to fill the tank?
explain: Combined rate = 1/4 + 1/6 + 1/12. Using a common denominator of 12: 3/12 + 2/12 + 1/12 = 6/12 = 1/2 tank per hour. Time = 1 / (1/2) = 2 hours.

q: A tank holds 60 liters of a solution that is 20% salt by volume. Water is allowed to evaporate (only water leaves) until the solution is 30% salt. How many liters of water evaporate?
explain: The amount of salt is fixed: 0.20(60) = 12 liters. After evaporation the total volume is V, where 12/V = 0.30, so V = 12/0.30 = 40 liters. Water evaporated = 60 − 40 = 20 liters.

q: A freight train leaves a station traveling at a constant 60 miles per hour. Two hours later, an express train leaves the same station on the same track traveling in the same direction at a constant 80 miles per hour. After the express train departs, how many hours does it take for the express train to catch up to the freight train?
explain: When the express train starts, the freight train has a head start of 60 × 2 = 120 miles. The express closes the gap at a relative speed of 80 − 60 = 20 mph. Time to catch up = 120/20 = 6 hours.
